If the target is ambiguous, ask before mutating state.\n- Treat permissions, member roles, and access as hard constraints.\n- Preserve document hierarchy rules: documents live in the document tree, not graph edges.\n- When context is incomplete, state the limit and use the narrowest tool that can fill the gap.\n\n## Capabilities, Skills, and Tools\n\nThink in three layers. They work together in sequence:\n\n1. Capability - what BuildOS can do for the user.\n2. Skill - workflow guidance for doing that work well. Skill metadata is preloaded in this prompt; call skill_load when the task is multi-step or easy to get wrong and you need the full markdown playbook.\n3. Tool / Op - the exact execution surface. The current tool names are listed later in Current Tool Surface.\n\nCapabilities:\n- Workspace and project overviews: Get BuildOS-native status snapshots for the whole workspace or one project without assembling generic ontology reads by hand.\n- Project creation: Turn a user idea into the smallest valid BuildOS project payload with inferred name, type, props, and only the initial structure the user actually described.\n- Project graph management: Inspect and update projects, goals, milestones, risks, and relationships across the BuildOS graph.\n- Planning and task structuring: Turn outcomes into plans and tasks, refine existing plans, and connect execution to goals, milestones, and documents.\n- Document workspace management: Create, update, place, and reorganize project documents and task workspace docs without breaking hierarchy rules.\n- Calendar management: Check the calendar, create or reschedule events, cancel events, and manage project calendar mapping.\n- People and profile context: Use user profile context and contact records when personalization or relationship context matters.\n- Project audit: Review project health, structure, blockers, stale work, and missing coverage from project context.\n- Project forecast: Establish likely schedule outcomes, slippage risk, and the strongest drivers of project uncertainty from project context.\n- Web research: Search the web, inspect URLs, and pull in current external information when needed.\n- BuildOS product reference: Explain BuildOS product concepts, usage patterns, and product-specific guidance from internal BuildOS reference tools.\n- Schema and field reference: Inspect field metadata and schema hints when exact model fields or contracts matter.\n\nSkill metadata:\n- calendar_management: Calendar workflow playbook for BuildOS agentic chat. Use for event reads/writes, scope decisions, and project calendar mapping.\n- document_workspace: Project document hierarchy playbook for doc tree operations, unlinked docs, task docs, and document CRUD rules.\n- libri_knowledge: Libri is BuildOS's connected durable library and enrichment system; use this playbook for Libri access questions, stable people or author knowledge, books, categories, authors, and ingested YouTube videos before generic web search.\n- people_context: People context playbook for profile lookup, contact search and updates, candidate resolution, and safe handling of sensitive contact values.\n- plan_management: BuildOS plan workflow playbook for turning a goal or milestone into a durable execution source of truth with scope, timeline, tasks, dependencies, owners, risks, and update rules.\n- project_audit: Project-scoped audit playbook for evaluating structure, documentation, timeline realism, velocity, loose work, duplication, blockers, and strategic gaps while staying in project context.\n- project_creation: Project creation playbook for turning a user idea into the smallest valid BuildOS project payload with inferred name, type_key, props, and only the initial structure the user actually described.\n- project_forecast: Project-scoped forecasting playbook for projecting trajectory, goal attainment, emerging work, schedule risk, assumptions, and next strategic moves while staying in project context.\n- task_management: Task workflow playbook for deciding when work should become a task and how to manage task scope, ownership, schedule, and relationships safely.\n\n## Current Focus and Purpose\n\nCurrent project focus:\n- Project: The Last Ember (bbc44a6f-b604-46e3-a6a7-dd931f5c0f13) (planning)\n- Project summary: A young blacksmith       "role": "user",
      "content": "Research notes for the magic system:\n\nFound interesting parallels with Japanese sword-making traditions - the idea of the smith's spirit entering the blade.               "description": "Optional BuildOS capability id or path such as \"overview\", \"project_creation\", or \"capabilities.calendar\". Prefer this when the capability family is already clear."

      "type": "function",

        "name": "change_chat_context",
        "parameters": {
          "type": "object",
          "required": [

          ],
          "properties": {

              "type": "string",
              "description": "Brief user-visible reason for the context change, grounded in the latest request."

            "target": {
              "enum": [

                "project"

              "type": "string",
              "description": "Target context. Use global for workspace/all-projects zoom-out. Use project for durable focus on one project."

            "project_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Exact project UUID when zooming into a known project."
            },
            "project_query": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Project name or phrase to resolve when zooming into a project and project_id is unknown."
            }
          }
        },
        "description": "Change the durable chat context when the latest user request should strategically zoom into one project or zoom out to the workspace.\n\nUse this early in the turn before answering when:\n- The user explicitly asks to zoom, switch, focus, go to a project, or go back to all projects.\n- The current context is global/workspace and the latest request is primarily about one identifiable project, so project tools should be loaded for the rest of the turn.\n- The current context is one project and the latest request is clearly about a different project, and the user appears to be moving focus rather than doing a brief comparison.\n- The user asks about all projects, workspace status, cross-project priorities, or wants to zoom out.\n\nDo not use this for ambiguous project names, one-off comparisons across multiple projects, or brief mentions that can be answered from current context. For project zoom-in, pass project_id if known or project_query if the name needs resolution. Ambiguous or missing project matches return candidates and do not change context."

              "maximum": 50,
              "description": "Maximum tasks to return"
            },
            "state_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Filter by state (todo, in_progress, blocked, done)"
            },
            "project_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Filter tasks by project ID"
            }
          }
        },
        "description": "List tasks from the ontology system (onto_tasks table). Returns abbreviated task information including id, title, state, and type.\nUse this for queries about ontology tasks, plans with tasks, or project task lists."
      },
      "parameters": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
          "limit": {
            "type": "number",
            "default": 20,
            "maximum": 50,
            "description": "Maximum tasks to return"
          },
          "state_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Filter by state (todo, in_progress, blocked, done)"
          },
          "project_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Filter tasks by project ID"
          }
        }
      },
      "description": "List tasks from the ontology system (onto_tasks table). Returns abbreviated task information including id, title, state, and type.\nUse this for queries about ontology tasks, plans with tasks, or project task lists."
    },
    {
      "name": "list_onto_documents",
      "type": "function",
      "function": {
        "name": "list_onto_documents",
        "parameters": {
          "type": "object",
          "properties": {
            "limit": {
              "type": "number",
              "default": 20,
              "maximum": 50,
              "description": "Maximum number of documents to return"
            },
            "type_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Filter by document type key (e.g., document.context.project, document.knowledge.research)"
            },
            "state_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Filter by document state (draft, in_review, ready, published, archived)"
            },
            "project_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Filter documents by project ID"
            }
          }
        },
        "description": "List documents from the ontology system (onto_documents table). Returns document summaries plus markdown heading outlines (H1/H2/H3), not full body content.\nUse get_onto_document_details when full document content is needed."
      },
      "parameters": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
          "limit": {
            "type": "number",
            "default": 20,
            "maximum": 50,
            "description": "Maximum number of documents to return"
          },
          "type_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Filter by document type key (e.g., document.context.project, document.knowledge.research)"
          },
          "state_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Filter by document state (draft, in_review, ready, published, archived)"
          },
          "project_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Filter documents by project ID"
          }
        }
      },
      "description": "List documents from the ontology system (onto_documents table). Returns document summaries plus markdown heading outlines (H1/H2/H3), not full body content.\nUse get_onto_document_details when full document content is needed."
    },
    {
      "name": "create_onto_task",
      "type": "function",
      "function": {
        "name": "create_onto_task",
        "parameters": {
          "type": "object",
          "required": [
            "project_id",
            "title"
          ],
          "properties": {
            "props": {
              "type": "object",
              "description": "Additional JSON properties"
            },
            "title": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Task title"
            },
            "due_at": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "ISO due date"
            },
            "parent": {
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "id": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "kind": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "is_primary": {
                  "type": "boolean"
                }
              },
              "description": "Optional primary containment parent"
            },
            "goal_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Optional goal UUID"
            },
            "parents": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {
                  "id": {
                    "type": "string"
                  },
                  "kind": {
                    "type": "string"
                  },
                  "is_primary": {
                    "type": "boolean"
                  }
                }
              },
              "description": "Optional containment parents"
            },
            "plan_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Optional plan UUID"
            },
            "priority": {
              "type": "number",
              "description": "Priority 1-5. Default 3."
            },
            "start_at": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "ISO start date"
            },
            "type_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "default": "task.execute",
              "description": "Task type key. Default task.execute."
            },
            "state_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "default": "todo",
              "description": "Initial state. Valid: todo, in_progress, blocked, done"
            },
            "project_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Project UUID"
            },
            "connections": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {
                  "id": {
                    "type": "string"
                  },
                  "rel": {
                    "type": "string"
                  },
                  "kind": {
                    "type": "string"
                  },
                  "intent": {
                    "enum": [
                      "containment",
                      "semantic"
                    ],
                    "type": "string"
                  }
                }
              },
              "description": "Optional semantic or containment links"
            },
            "description": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Task description"
            },
            "assignee_handles": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {
                "type": "string"
              },
              "description": "Active project member handles, e.g. @dj."
            },
            "assignee_actor_ids": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {
                "type": "string"
              },
              "description": "Active project member actor UUIDs. Max 10."
            },
            "supporting_milestone_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Optional milestone UUID"
            }
          },
          "additionalProperties": false
        },
        "description": "Create a new task in the ontology system.\nCreates a task within a project and optionally assigns it to a plan and collaborators.\nUse when the user explicitly asks to add/track/remind, or when the item is future human work outside this chat.\nDo not create tasks for research, brainstorming, summarizing, or drafting you can do now; do the work instead.\nAlways include project_id and a concrete title. Link to parent/plan/goal/milestone only when known. Use assignees only for known project members.\nLoad task_management for complex task flows."
      },
      "parameters": {
        "type": "object",
        "required": [
          "project_id",
          "title"
        ],
        "properties": {
          "props": {
            "type": "object",
            "description": "Additional JSON properties"
          },
          "title": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Task title"
          },
          "due_at": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "ISO due date"
          },
          "parent": {
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
              "id": {
                "type": "string"
              },
              "kind": {
                "type": "string"
              },
              "is_primary": {
                "type": "boolean"
              }
            },
            "description": "Optional primary containment parent"
          },
          "goal_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Optional goal UUID"
          },
          "parents": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "id": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "kind": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "is_primary": {
                  "type": "boolean"
                }
              }
            },
            "description": "Optional containment parents"
          },
          "plan_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Optional plan UUID"
          },
          "priority": {
            "type": "number",
            "description": "Priority 1-5. Default 3."
          },
          "start_at": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "ISO start date"
          },
          "type_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "default": "task.execute",
            "description": "Task type key. Default task.execute."
          },
          "state_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "default": "todo",
            "description": "Initial state. Valid: todo, in_progress, blocked, done"
          },
          "project_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Project UUID"
          },
          "connections": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "id": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "rel": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "kind": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "intent": {
                  "enum": [
                    "containment",
                    "semantic"
                  ],
                  "type": "string"
                }
              }
            },
            "description": "Optional semantic or containment links"
          },
          "description": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Task description"
          },
          "assignee_handles": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
              "type": "string"
            },
            "description": "Active project member handles, e.g. @dj."
          },
          "assignee_actor_ids": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
              "type": "string"
            },
            "description": "Active project member actor UUIDs. Max 10."
          },
          "supporting_milestone_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Optional milestone UUID"
          }
        },
        "additionalProperties": false
      },
      "description": "Create a new task in the ontology system.\nCreates a task within a project and optionally assigns it to a plan and collaborators.\nUse when the user explicitly asks to add/track/remind, or when the item is future human work outside this chat.\nDo not create tasks for research, brainstorming, summarizing, or drafting you can do now; do the work instead.\nAlways include project_id and a concrete title. Link to parent/plan/goal/milestone only when known. Use assignees only for known project members.\nLoad task_management for complex task flows."
    },
    {
      "name": "update_onto_task",
      "type": "function",
      "function": {
        "name": "update_onto_task",
        "parameters": {
          "type": "object",
          "required": [
            "task_id"
          ],
          "properties": {
            "props": {
              "type": "object",
              "description": "Properties to merge with existing props"
            },
            "title": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "New task title"
            },
            "due_at": {
              "type": [
                "string",
                "null"
              ],
              "description": "New due date (ISO format) or null to clear"
            },
            "goal_id": {
              "type": [
                "string",
                "null"
              ],
              "description": "Optional goal UUID that this task supports (null clears)"
            },
            "task_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Task UUID (required)"
            },
            "priority": {
              "type": "number",
              "description": "New priority (1-5)"
            },
            "start_at": {
              "type": [
                "string",
                "null"
              ],
              "description": "New start date (ISO format) or null to clear"
            },
            "type_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Work mode taxonomy: task.{work_mode}[.{specialization}]. Modes: execute, create, refine, research, review, coordinate, admin, plan."
            },
            "state_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "New state (todo, in_progress, blocked, done)"
            },
            "project_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Optional project UUID used for assignee handle resolution. If omitted, the task project is resolved automatically."
            },
            "description": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "New description"
            },
            "update_strategy": {
              "enum": [
                "replace",
                "append",
                "merge_llm"
              ],
              "type": "string",
              "description": "How to apply description updates: 'replace' (default), 'append', or 'merge_llm' to intelligently merge with existing description."
            },
            "assignee_handles": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {
                "type": "string"
              },
              "description": "Optional assignee handles like [\"@jim\"]. Resolved against active project members by name/email local-part."
            },
            "assignee_actor_ids": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {
                "type": "string"
              },
              "description": "Optional full replacement assignee actor ID list (UUIDs) for active project members only. Prefer assignee_handles unless IDs were just retrieved from project members. Use [] to clear assignees. Max 10."
            },
            "merge_instructions": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Optional guidance when merging description text (e.g., keep bullets, integrate notes). Used with append/merge_llm."
            },
            "supporting_milestone_id": {
              "type": [
                "string",
                "null"
              ],
              "description": "Optional milestone UUID this task targets (null clears)"
            }
          }
        },
        "description": "Update an existing task in the ontology system.\nCan modify title, description, state, priority, assignees, scheduling, and custom properties.\nOnly updates fields that are provided - omitted fields remain unchanged."
      },
      "parameters": {
        "type": "object",
        "required": [
          "task_id"
        ],
        "properties": {
          "props": {
            "type": "object",
            "description": "Properties to merge with existing props"
          },
          "title": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "New task title"
          },
          "due_at": {
            "type": [
              "string",
              "null"
            ],
            "description": "New due date (ISO format) or null to clear"
          },
          "goal_id": {
            "type": [
              "string",
              "null"
            ],
            "description": "Optional goal UUID that this task supports (null clears)"
          },
          "task_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Task UUID (required)"
          },
          "priority": {
            "type": "number",
            "description": "New priority (1-5)"
          },
          "start_at": {
            "type": [
              "string",
              "null"
            ],
            "description": "New start date (ISO format) or null to clear"
          },
          "type_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Work mode taxonomy: task.{work_mode}[.{specialization}]. Modes: execute, create, refine, research, review, coordinate, admin, plan."
          },
          "state_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "New state (todo, in_progress, blocked, done)"
          },
          "project_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Optional project UUID used for assignee handle resolution. If omitted, the task project is resolved automatically."
          },
          "description": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "New description"
          },
          "update_strategy": {
            "enum": [
              "replace",
              "append",
              "merge_llm"
            ],
            "type": "string",
            "description": "How to apply description updates: 'replace' (default), 'append', or 'merge_llm' to intelligently merge with existing description."
          },
          "assignee_handles": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
              "type": "string"
            },
            "description": "Optional assignee handles like [\"@jim\"]. Resolved against active project members by name/email local-part."
          },
          "assignee_actor_ids": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
              "type": "string"
            },
            "description": "Optional full replacement assignee actor ID list (UUIDs) for active project members only. Prefer assignee_handles unless IDs were just retrieved from project members. Use [] to clear assignees. Max 10."
          },
          "merge_instructions": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Optional guidance when merging description text (e.g., keep bullets, integrate notes). Used with append/merge_llm."
          },
          "supporting_milestone_id": {
            "type": [
              "string",
              "null"
            ],
            "description": "Optional milestone UUID this task targets (null clears)"
          }
        }
      },
      "description": "Update an existing task in the ontology system.\nCan modify title, description, state, priority, assignees, scheduling, and custom properties.\nOnly updates fields that are provided - omitted fields remain unchanged."
    },
    {
      "name": "create_onto_document",
      "type": "function",
      "function": {
        "name": "create_onto_document",
        "parameters": {
          "type": "object",
          "required": [
            "project_id",
            "title",
            "description"
          ],
          "properties": {
            "props": {
              "type": "object",
              "description": "Additional properties/metadata"
            },
            "title": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Document title (required)"
            },
            "parent": {
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "id": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "kind": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "is_primary": {
                  "type": "boolean"
                }
              },
              "description": "Optional parent reference for semantic linking (legacy)"
            },
            "content": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Markdown body content stored in the content column"
            },
            "parents": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {
                  "id": {
                    "type": "string"
                  },
                  "kind": {
                    "type": "string"
                  },
                  "is_primary": {
                    "type": "boolean"
                  }
                }
              },
              "description": "Optional multiple semantic parents (legacy)"
            },
            "position": {
              "type": "number",
              "description": "Position among siblings (0-indexed). Omit to place at end."
            },
            "type_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "default": "document.default",
              "description": "Document type taxonomy (required): document.{family}[.{variant}]\nFamilies: context (project/brief), knowledge (research/brain_dump), notes (meeting_notes/rfc), spec (product/technical), reference (handbook/sop), intake (client/project).\nExamples: document.context.project, document.knowledge.research, document.spec.technical"
            },
            "parent_id": {
              "type": [
                "string",
                "null"
              ],
              "description": "Parent document ID to place this document under in the tree hierarchy. Null or omitted places at root level."
            },
            "state_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "default": "draft",
              "description": "Document state (draft, in_review, ready, published, archived)"
            },
            "project_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Project UUID (required)"
            },
            "description": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Short summary of the document (required)"
            }
          }
        },
        "description": "Create a new document in the ontology system.\nUse for briefs, specs, context docs, or research artifacts linked to a project.\nDocuments are organized in a hierarchical tree structure. Use parent_id to place the document under a parent folder."
      },
      "parameters": {
        "type": "object",
        "required": [
          "project_id",
          "title",
          "description"
        ],
        "properties": {
          "props": {
            "type": "object",
            "description": "Additional properties/metadata"
          },
          "title": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Document title (required)"
          },
          "parent": {
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
              "id": {
                "type": "string"
              },
              "kind": {
                "type": "string"
              },
              "is_primary": {
                "type": "boolean"
              }
            },
            "description": "Optional parent reference for semantic linking (legacy)"
          },
          "content": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Markdown body content stored in the content column"
          },
          "parents": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "id": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "kind": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "is_primary": {
                  "type": "boolean"
                }
              }
            },
            "description": "Optional multiple semantic parents (legacy)"
          },
          "position": {
            "type": "number",
            "description": "Position among siblings (0-indexed). Omit to place at end."
          },
          "type_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "default": "document.default",
            "description": "Document type taxonomy (required): document.{family}[.{variant}]\nFamilies: context (project/brief), knowledge (research/brain_dump), notes (meeting_notes/rfc), spec (product/technical), reference (handbook/sop), intake (client/project).\nExamples: document.context.project, document.knowledge.research, document.spec.technical"
          },
          "parent_id": {
            "type": [
              "string",
              "null"
            ],
            "description": "Parent document ID to place this document under in the tree hierarchy. Null or omitted places at root level."
          },
          "state_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "default": "draft",
            "description": "Document state (draft, in_review, ready, published, archived)"
          },
          "project_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Project UUID (required)"
          },
          "description": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Short summary of the document (required)"
          }
        }
      },
      "description": "Create a new document in the ontology system.\nUse for briefs, specs, context docs, or research artifacts linked to a project.\nDocuments are organized in a hierarchical tree structure. Use parent_id to place the document under a parent folder."
    },
    {
      "name": "update_onto_document",
      "type": "function",
      "function": {
        "name": "update_onto_document",
        "parameters": {
          "type": "object",
          "required": [
            "document_id"
          ],
          "properties": {
            "props": {
              "type": "object",
              "description": "Metadata fields to merge into document props"
            },
            "title": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "New document title"
            },
            "content": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Markdown content to store in the content column. Required when update_strategy is append or merge_llm."
            },
            "type_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "New document type key"
            },
            "state_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Document state (draft, in_review, ready, published, archived)"
            },
            "description": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Short summary of the document"
            },
            "document_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Document UUID (required)"
            },
            "update_strategy": {
              "enum": [
                "replace",
                "append",
                "merge_llm"
              ],
              "type": "string",
              "default": "replace",
              "description": "How to apply content: 'replace' (default), 'append', or 'merge_llm' to intelligently merge with existing content."
            },
            "merge_instructions": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Optional guidance when merging content (e.g., keep headers, preserve tables, integrate research notes). Used with append/merge_llm, but does not replace the required content."
            }
          },
          "additionalProperties": false
        },
        "description": "Update an existing ontology document. Use for edits to titles, states, body markdown, or metadata. For update_strategy \"append\" or \"merge_llm\", include non-empty content/body_markdown/markdown/body/text. merge_instructions alone is not content. Append example: update_onto_document({ document_id, content: \"## Progress Updates\\n\\n- Chapter 2 complete...\", update_strategy: \"append\", merge_instructions: \"Append under Progress Updates; preserve existing sections.\" })"
      },
      "parameters": {
        "type": "object",
        "required": [
          "document_id"
        ],
        "properties": {
          "props": {
            "type": "object",
            "description": "Metadata fields to merge into document props"
          },
          "title": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "New document title"
          },
          "content": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Markdown content to store in the content column. Required when update_strategy is append or merge_llm."
          },
          "type_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "New document type key"
          },
          "state_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Document state (draft, in_review, ready, published, archived)"
          },
          "description": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Short summary of the document"
          },
          "document_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Document UUID (required)"
          },
          "update_strategy": {
            "enum": [
              "replace",
              "append",
              "merge_llm"
            ],
            "type": "string",
            "default": "replace",
            "description": "How to apply content: 'replace' (default), 'append', or 'merge_llm' to intelligently merge with existing content."
          },
          "merge_instructions": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Optional guidance when merging content (e.g., keep headers, preserve tables, integrate research notes). Used with append/merge_llm, but does not replace the required content."
          }
        },
        "additionalProperties": false
      },
      "description": "Update an existing ontology document. Use for edits to titles, states, body markdown, or metadata. For update_strategy \"append\" or \"merge_llm\", include non-empty content/body_markdown/markdown/body/text. merge_instructions alone is not content. Append example: update_onto_document({ document_id, content: \"## Progress Updates\\n\\n- Chapter 2 complete...\", update_strategy: \"append\", merge_instructions: \"Append under Progress Updates; preserve existing sections.\" })"
    }
  ],

    "lite_sections": [
      {
        "id": "identity_mission",
        "kind": "static",
        "chars": 602,
        "title": "Identity and Mission",
        "source": "lite.static_frame",
        "content": "Who:\n- You are a proactive project assistant for BuildOS, working for the signed-in user.\n- BuildOS is a graph-based project collaboration system. Projects can contain goals, milestones, plans, tasks, documents, risks, events, members, and relationships.\n\nMission:\n- Help users capture, organize, understand, and advance their project work.\n- Preserve concrete user details, ground answers in available context, and use tools when the answer or action requires current project data.\n- Keep the conversation useful for whatever the user says next; do not overfit the seed prompt to one expected request.",
        "estimatedTokens": 151
      },
      {
        "id": "operating_strategy",
        "kind": "static",
        "chars": 1019,
        "title": "Operating Strategy",
        "source": "lite.strategy",
        "content": "How to act:\n- Start with the loaded context. If the loaded context is enough, answer without extra tool calls.\n- Treat context zooming as durable state movement. Use change_chat_context early when the latest request should zoom into one resolved project or back out to the workspace.\n- Do not bounce contexts for ambiguous project names, multi-project comparisons, or brief side mentions. Resolve ambiguity first, then shift only when the intended focus is clear.\n- Use direct tools first when they fit. Use discovery tools only when the exact operation or schema is missing.\n- Load a skill when the workflow is multi-step, stateful, or easy to get wrong; do not load full skill playbooks for simple answers.\n- Ask one concise clarification only when the missing detail blocks a safe answer or write.\n- After a tool call, anchor the next step in what the tool actually returned: what changed, where the runtime is now, and what should happen next.\n- Keep scratch reasoning private and make user-facing responses direct.",
        "estimatedTokens": 255
      },
      {
        "id": "safety_data_rules",
        "kind": "static",
        "chars": 1326,
        "title": "Safety and Data Rules",
        "source": "lite.safety",
        "content": "- Do not claim a tool ran unless the runtime supplied a successful tool result.\n- Discovering a tool, loading a schema, reading context, or planning is not completion. Only say an entity was created, updated, moved, merged, archived, deleted, scheduled, or linked after the corresponding write tool succeeded.\n- If any write fails and no later retry repairs the same target, state what did not persist and keep the partial-success summary precise.\n- Do not claim a requested document type, tree placement, link, or cross-link unless the successful tool result confirms the actual type or operation.\n- Never copy internal tool-call syntax into durable fields. User-visible titles, descriptions, document content, project descriptions, and props must contain final content only, not tags like <parameter>, <tool_call>, or argument fragments.\n- Do not invent project, task, document, calendar, member, or Libri data that is not in loaded context or tool results.\n- For writes, use exact IDs from context or tool results. If the target is ambiguous, ask before mutating state.\n- Treat permissions, member roles, and access as hard constraints.\n- Preserve document hierarchy rules: documents live in the document tree, not graph edges.\n- When context is incomplete, state the limit and use the narrowest tool that can fill the gap.",
        "estimatedTokens": 332
      },
      {
        "id": "capabilities_skills_tools",
        "kind": "static",
        "chars": 3767,
        "title": "Capabilities, Skills, and Tools",
        "source": "lite.static_capability_skill_catalog",
        "content": "Think in three layers. They work together in sequence:\n\n1. Capability - what BuildOS can do for the user.\n2. Skill - workflow guidance for doing that work well. Skill metadata is preloaded in this prompt; call skill_load when the task is multi-step or easy to get wrong and you need the full markdown playbook.\n3. Tool / Op - the exact execution surface. The current tool names are listed later in Current Tool Surface.\n\nCapabilities:\n- Workspace and project overviews: Get BuildOS-native status snapshots for the whole workspace or one project without assembling generic ontology reads by hand.\n- Project creation: Turn a user idea into the smallest valid BuildOS project payload with inferred name, type, props, and only the initial structure the user actually described.\n- Project graph management: Inspect and update projects, goals, milestones, risks, and relationships across the BuildOS graph.\n- Planning and task structuring: Turn outcomes into plans and tasks, refine existing plans, and connect execution to goals, milestones, and documents.\n- Document workspace management: Create, update, place, and reorganize project documents and task workspace docs without breaking hierarchy rules.\n- Calendar management: Check the calendar, create or reschedule events, cancel events, and manage project calendar mapping.\n- People and profile context: Use user profile context and contact records when personalization or relationship context matters.\n- Project audit: Review project health, structure, blockers, stale work, and missing coverage from project context.\n- Project forecast: Establish likely schedule outcomes, slippage risk, and the strongest drivers of project uncertainty from project context.\n- Web research: Search the web, inspect URLs, and pull in current external information when needed.\n- BuildOS product reference: Explain BuildOS product concepts, usage patterns, and product-specific guidance from internal BuildOS reference tools.\n- Schema and field reference: Inspect field metadata and schema hints when exact model fields or contracts matter.\n\nSkill metadata:\n- calendar_management: Calendar workflow playbook for BuildOS agentic chat. Use for event reads/writes, scope decisions, and project calendar mapping.\n- document_workspace: Project document hierarchy playbook for doc tree operations, unlinked docs, task docs, and document CRUD rules.\n- libri_knowledge: Libri is BuildOS's connected durable library and enrichment system; use this playbook for Libri access questions, stable people or author knowledge, books, categories, authors, and ingested YouTube videos before generic web search.\n- people_context: People context playbook for profile lookup, contact search and updates, candidate resolution, and safe handling of sensitive contact values.\n- plan_management: BuildOS plan workflow playbook for turning a goal or milestone into a durable execution source of truth with scope, timeline, tasks, dependencies, owners, risks, and update rules.\n- project_audit: Project-scoped audit playbook for evaluating structure, documentation, timeline realism, velocity, loose work, duplication, blockers, and strategic gaps while staying in project context.\n- project_creation: Project creation playbook for turning a user idea into the smallest valid BuildOS project payload with inferred name, type_key, props, and only the initial structure the user actually described.\n- project_forecast: Project-scoped forecasting playbook for projecting trajectory, goal attainment, emerging work, schedule risk, assumptions, and next strategic moves while staying in project context.\n- task_management: Task workflow playbook for deciding when work should become a task and how to manage task scope, ownership, schedule, and relationships safely.",
        "estimatedTokens": 942
      },
      {

        "content": "Current project focus:\n- Project: The Last Ember (bbc44a6f-b604-46e3-a6a7-dd931f5c0f13) (planning)\n- Project summary: A young blacksmith discovers she can forge magical weapons when the kingdom's last dragon dies and darkness threatens the realm.\n- Primary goal: \"Write the fantasy novel 'The Last Ember'\" (draft)\n- Focus entity: none\n\nUse this seed for:\n- Seed a project-scoped assistant that understands the current project and can help move its work forward.",
        "estimatedTokens": 116
      },

Projects can contain goals, milestones, plans, tasks, documents, risks, events, members, and relationships.\n\nMission:\n- Help users capture, organize, understand, and advance their project work.\n- Preserve concrete user details, ground answers in available context, and use tools when the answer or action requires current project data.\n- Keep the conversation useful for whatever the user says next; do not overfit the seed prompt to one expected request.\n\n## Operating Strategy\n\nHow to act:\n- Start with the loaded context. If the loaded context is enough, answer without extra tool calls.\n- Treat context zooming as durable state movement. Use change_chat_context early when the latest request should zoom into one resolved project or back out to the workspace.\n- Do not bounce contexts for ambiguous project names, multi-project comparisons, or brief side mentions. Resolve ambiguity first, then shift only when the intended focus is clear.\n- Use direct tools first when they fit. Use discovery tools only when the exact operation or schema is missing.\n- Load a skill when the workflow is multi-step, stateful, or easy to get wrong; do not load full skill playbooks for simple answers.\n- Ask one concise clarification only when the missing detail blocks a safe answer or write.\n- After a tool call, anchor the next step in what the tool actually returned: what changed, where the runtime is now, and what should happen next.\n- Keep scratch reasoning private and make user-facing responses direct.\n\n## Safety and Data Rules\n\n- Do not claim a tool ran unless the runtime supplied a successful tool result.\n- Discovering a tool, loading a schema, reading context, or planning is not completion. Only say an entity was created, updated, moved, merged, archived, deleted, scheduled, or linked after the corresponding write tool succeeded.\n- If any write fails and no later retry repairs the same target, state what did not persist and keep the partial-success summary precise.\n- Do not claim a requested document type, tree placement, link, or cross-link unless the successful tool result confirms the actual type or operation.\n- Never copy internal tool-call syntax into durable fields. User-visible titles, descriptions, document content, project descriptions, and props must contain final content only, not tags like <parameter>, <tool_call>, or argument fragments.\n- Do not invent project, task, document, calendar, member, or Libri data that is not in loaded context or tool results.\n- For writes, use exact IDs from context or tool results. If the target is ambiguous, ask before mutating state.\n- Treat permissions, member roles, and access as hard constraints.\n- Preserve document hierarchy rules: documents live in the document tree, not graph edges.\n- When context is incomplete, state the limit and use the narrowest tool that can fill the gap.\n\n## Capabilities, Skills, and Tools\n\nThink in three layers. They work together in sequence:\n\n1. Capability - what BuildOS can do for the user.\n2. Skill - workflow guidance for doing that work well. Skill metadata is preloaded in this prompt; call skill_load when the task is multi-step or easy to get wrong and you need the full markdown playbook.\n3. Tool / Op - the exact execution surface. The current tool names are listed later in Current Tool Surface.\n\nCapabilities:\n- Workspace and project overviews: Get BuildOS-native status snapshots for the whole workspace or one project without assembling generic ontology reads by hand.\n- Project creation: Turn a user idea into the smallest valid BuildOS project payload with inferred name, type, props, and only the initial structure the user actually described.\n- Project graph management: Inspect and update projects, goals, milestones, risks, and relationships across the BuildOS graph.\n- Planning and task structuring: Turn outcomes into plans and tasks, refine existing plans, and connect execution to goals, milestones, and documents.\n- Document workspace management: Create, update, place, and reorganize project documents and task workspace docs without breaking hierarchy rules.\n- Calendar management: Check the calendar, create or reschedule events, cancel events, and manage project calendar mapping.\n- People and profile context: Use user profile context and contact records when personalization or relationship context matters.\n- Project audit: Review project health, structure, blockers, stale work, and missing coverage from project context.\n- Project forecast: Establish likely schedule outcomes, slippage risk, and the strongest drivers of project uncertainty from project context.\n- Web research: Search the web, inspect URLs, and pull in current external information when needed.\n- BuildOS product reference: Explain BuildOS product concepts, usage patterns, and product-specific guidance from internal BuildOS reference tools.\n- Schema and field reference: Inspect field metadata and schema hints when exact model fields or contracts matter.\n\nSkill metadata:\n- calendar_management: Calendar workflow playbook for BuildOS agentic chat. Use for event reads/writes, scope decisions, and project calendar mapping.\n- document_workspace: Project document hierarchy playbook for doc tree operations, unlinked docs, task docs, and document CRUD rules.\n- libri_knowledge: Libri is BuildOS's connected durable library and enrichment system; use this playbook for Libri access questions, stable people or author knowledge, books, categories, authors, and ingested YouTube videos before generic web search.\n- people_context: People context playbook for profile lookup, contact search and updates, candidate resolution, and safe handling of sensitive contact values.\n- plan_management: BuildOS plan workflow playbook for turning a goal or milestone into a durable execution source of truth with scope, timeline, tasks, dependencies, owners, risks, and update rules.\n- project_audit: Project-scoped audit playbook for evaluating structure, documentation, timeline realism, velocity, loose work, duplication, blockers, and strategic gaps while staying in project context.\n- project_creation: Project creation playbook for turning a user idea into the smallest valid BuildOS project payload with inferred name, type_key, props, and only the initial structure the user actually described.\n- project_forecast: Project-scoped forecasting playbook for projecting trajectory, goal attainment, emerging work, schedule risk, assumptions, and next strategic moves while staying in project context.\n- task_management: Task workflow playbook for deciding when work should become a task and how to manage task scope, ownership, schedule, and relationships safely.\n\n## Current Focus and Purpose\n\nCurrent project focus:\n- Project: The Last Ember (bbc44a6f-b604-46e3-a6a7-dd931f5c0f13) (planning)\n- Project summary: A young blacksmith discovers she can forge magical weapons when the kingdom's last dragon dies and darkness threatens the realm.\n- Primary goal: \"Write the fantasy novel 'The Last Ember'\" (draft)\n- Focus entity: none\n\nUse this seed for:\n- Seed a project-scoped assistant that understands the current project and can help move its work forward.\n\n## Location and Loaded Context\n\nLoaded scope:\n- inside The MESSAGES\n────────────────────────────────────────\n[1] role=system\n# BuildOS Lite Agentic Chat Prompt\n\n\n\nPrompt variant: lite_seed_v1\n\n\n\n## Identity and Mission\n\nWho:\n- You are a proactive project assistant for BuildOS, working for the signed-in user.\n- BuildOS is a graph-based project collaboration system. Projects can contain goals, milestones, plans, tasks, documents, risks, events, members, and relationships.\n\nMission:\n- Help users capture, organize, understand, and advance their project work.\n- Preserve concrete user details, ground answers in available context, and use tools when the answer or action requires current project data.\n- Keep the conversation useful for whatever the user says next; do not overfit the seed prompt to one expected request.\n\n## Operating Strategy\n\nHow to act:\n- Start with the loaded context. If the loaded context is enough, answer without extra tool calls.\n- Treat context zooming as durable state movement. Use change_chat_context early when the latest request should zoom into one resolved project or back out to the workspace.\n- Do not bounce contexts for ambiguous project names, multi-project comparisons, or brief side mentions. Resolve ambiguity first, then shift only when the intended focus is clear.\n- Use direct tools first when they fit. Use discovery tools only when the exact operation or schema is missing.\n- Load a skill when the workflow is multi-step, stateful, or easy to get wrong; do not load full skill playbooks for simple answers.\n- Ask one concise clarification only when the missing detail blocks a safe answer or write.\n- After a tool call, anchor the next step in what the tool actually returned: what changed, where the runtime is now, and what should happen next.\n- Keep scratch reasoning private and make user-facing responses direct.\n\n## Safety and Data Rules\n\n- Do not claim a tool ran unless the runtime supplied a successful tool result.\n- Discovering a tool, loading a schema, reading context, or planning is not completion. Only say an entity was created, updated, moved, merged, archived, deleted, scheduled, or linked after the corresponding write tool succeeded.\n- If any write fails and no later retry repairs the same target, state what did not persist and keep the partial-success summary precise.\n- Do not claim a requested document type, tree placement, link, or cross-link unless the successful tool result confirms the actual type or operation.\n- Never copy internal tool-call syntax into durable fields. User-visible titles, descriptions, document content, project descriptions, and props must contain final content only, not tags like <parameter>, <tool_call>, or argument fragments.\n- Do not invent project, task, document, calendar, member, or Libri data that is not in loaded context or tool results.\n- For writes, use exact IDs from context or tool results. If the target is ambiguous, ask before mutating state.\n- Treat permissions, member roles, and access as hard constraints.\n- Preserve document hierarchy rules: documents live in the document tree, not graph edges.\n- When context is incomplete, state the limit and use the narrowest tool that can fill the gap.\n\n## Capabilities, Skills, and Tools\n\nThink in three layers. They work together in sequence:\n\n1. Capability - what BuildOS can do for the user.\n2. Skill - workflow guidance for doing that work well. Skill metadata is preloaded in this prompt; call skill_load when the task is multi-step or easy to get wrong and you need the full markdown playbook.\n3. Tool / Op - the exact execution surface. The current tool names are listed later in Current Tool Surface.\n\nCapabilities:\n- Workspace and project overviews: Get BuildOS-native status snapshots for the whole workspace or one project without assembling generic ontology reads by hand.\n- Project creation: Turn a user idea into the smallest valid BuildOS project payload with inferred name, type, props, and only the initial structure the user actually described.\n- Project graph management: Inspect and update projects, goals, milestones, risks, and relationships across the BuildOS graph.\n- Planning and task structuring: Turn outcomes into plans and tasks, refine existing plans, and connect execution to goals, milestones, and documents.\n- Document workspace management: Create, update, place, and reorganize project documents and task workspace docs without breaking hierarchy rules.\n- Calendar management: Check the calendar, create or reschedule events, cancel events, and manage project calendar mapping.\n- People and profile context: Use user profile context and contact records when personalization or relationship context matters.\n- Project audit: Review project health, structure, blockers, stale work, and missing coverage from project context.\n- Project forecast: Establish likely schedule outcomes, slippage risk, and the strongest drivers of project uncertainty from project context.\n- Web research: Search the web, inspect URLs, and pull in current external information when needed.\n- BuildOS product reference: Explain BuildOS product concepts, usage patterns, and product-specific guidance from internal BuildOS reference tools.\n- Schema and field reference: Inspect field metadata and schema hints when exact model fields or contracts matter.\n\nSkill metadata:\n- calendar_management: Calendar workflow playbook for BuildOS agentic chat. Use for event reads/writes, scope decisions, and project calendar mapping.\n- document_workspace: Project document hierarchy playbook for doc tree operations, unlinked docs, task docs, and document CRUD rules.\n- libri_knowledge: Libri is BuildOS's connected durable library and enrichment system; use this playbook for Libri access questions, stable people or author knowledge, books, categories, authors, and ingested YouTube videos before generic web search.\n- people_context: People context playbook for profile lookup, contact search and updates, candidate resolution, and safe handling of sensitive contact values.\n- plan_management: BuildOS plan workflow playbook for turning a goal or milestone into a durable execution source of truth with scope, timeline, tasks, dependencies, owners, risks, and update rules.\n- project_audit: Project-scoped audit playbook for evaluating structure, documentation, timeline realism, velocity, loose work, duplication, blockers, and strategic gaps while staying in project context.\n- project_creation: Project creation playbook for turning a user idea into the smallest valid BuildOS project payload with inferred name, type_key, props, and only the initial structure the user actually described.\n- project_forecast: Project-scoped forecasting playbook for projecting trajectory, goal attainment, emerging work, schedule risk, assumptions, and next strategic moves while staying in project context.\n- task_management: Task workflow playbook for deciding when work should become a task and how to manage task scope, ownership, schedule, and relationships safely.\n\n## Current Focus and Purpose\n\nCurrent project focus:\n- Project: The Last Ember (bbc44a6f-b604-46e3-a6a7-dd931f5c0f13) (planning)\n- Project summary: A young blacksmith discovers she can forge magical weapons when the kingdom's last dragon dies and darkness threatens the realm.\n- Primary goal: \"Write the fantasy novel 'The Last Ember'\" (draft)\n- Focus entity: none\n\nUse this seed for:\n- Seed a project-scoped assistant that understands the current project and can help move its work forward.\n\n## Location and Loaded Context\n\nLoaded scope:\n- inside The       "tool_arguments": {
        "title": "Magic System Research Notes",
        "content": "## Magic System Research Notes\n\nFound interesting parallels with Japanese sword-making traditions - the idea of the smith's spirit entering the blade. Could adapt this: Elena's emotions during forging affect the weapon's properties.\n\n- Anger = fire damage\n- Sorrow = ice/frost\n- Joy = healing properties\n- Fear = defensive shields\n\n### Also researching:\n- Damascus steel patterns for visual descriptions\n- Celtic mythology about smith gods (Goibniu)\n- Types of medieval weapons beyond swords\n\n### World-building additions:\n- **The Forge Temples**: ancient sites where dragon fire still burns\n- **Smith's Guild** hierarchy and traditions\n- **The Quenching Ritual**: how magical weapons are completed\n- **Regional differences** in forging techniques across Aethermoor",
        "type_key": "document.knowledge.research",
        "state_key": "draft",
        "project_id": "bbc44a6f-b604-46e3-a6a7-dd931f5c0f13",
        "description": "Research notes on the magic system for 'The Last Ember': emotions affecting forged weapons, inspirations from Japanese sword-making, Damascus steel, Celtic mythology, and world-building elements like Forge Temples, Smith's Guild, Quenching Ritual, and regional forging differences."
      },
      "tool_result": {
        "message": "Created ontology document \"Magic System Research Notes\"",

          "id": "ce9f631d-fac5-45b9-8cf5-e9d91449f9e5",
          "props": {
            "body_markdown": "## Magic System Research Notes\n\nFound interesting parallels with Japanese sword-making traditions - the idea of the smith's spirit entering the blade. Could adapt this: Elena's emotions during forging affect the weapon's properties.\n\n- Anger = fire damage\n- Sorrow = ice/frost\n- Joy = healing properties\n- Fear = defensive shields\n\n### Also researching:\n- Damascus steel patterns for visual descriptions\n- Celtic mythology about smith gods (Goibniu)\n- Types of medieval weapons beyond swords\n\n### World-building additions:\n- **The Forge Temples**: ancient sites where dragon fire still burns\n- **Smith's Guild** hierarchy and traditions\n- **The Quenching Ritual**: how magical weapons are completed\n- **Regional differences** in forging techniques across Aethermoor"
          },

    "arguments": {
      "title": "Magic System Research Notes",
      "content": "## Magic System Research Notes\n\nFound interesting parallels with Japanese sword-making traditions - the idea of the smith's spirit entering the blade. Could adapt this: Elena's emotions during forging affect the weapon's properties.\n\n- Anger = fire damage\n- Sorrow = ice/frost\n- Joy = healing properties\n- Fear = defensive shields\n\n### Also researching:\n- Damascus steel patterns for visual descriptions\n- Celtic mythology about smith gods (Goibniu)\n- Types of medieval weapons beyond swords\n\n### World-building additions:\n- **The Forge Temples**: ancient sites where dragon fire still burns\n- **Smith's Guild** hierarchy and traditions\n- **The Quenching Ritual**: how magical weapons are completed\n- **Regional differences** in forging techniques across Aethermoor",
      "type_key": "document.knowledge.research",
      "state_key": "draft",
      "project_id": "bbc44a6f-b604-46e3-a6a7-dd931f5c0f13",
      "description": "Research notes on the magic system for 'The Last Ember': emotions affecting forged weapons, inspirations from Japanese sword-making, Damascus steel, Celtic mythology, and world-building elements like Forge Temples, Smith's Guild, Quenching Ritual, and regional forging differences."
    },
